explain.title: Explain
explain.fullScan: {n} full table scan(s) — consider adding an index
explain.hint: A SCAN without an index reads every row. Add an index on the filtered/joined columns.
explain.tempBtree: Query builds a temporary B-tree to sort (ORDER BY/GROUP BY) — an index on those columns avoids the sort.
explain.selectStar: SELECT * fetches every column — select only what you need.
explain.noLimit: No LIMIT — large result sets transfer every matching row.
explain.likeWild: LIKE with a leading % cannot use an index.
explain.nonSargable: A function wrapping a column in WHERE prevents index use.
explain.orUsage: OR in WHERE often prevents index use — consider splitting into UNION or an IN (...) list.
explain.multiScan: Multiple table scans — a join may be missing an index, risking a near-cartesian scan.
explain.staleStats: No query-planner statistics — run ANALYZE so the planner picks better indexes.
explain.bnNone: No obvious bottlenecks in the bytecode.
explain.opOpen: Open a cursor on a table or index
explain.opSort: Temporary sort — an index on these columns would avoid it
explain.opAutoindex: Building an automatic index — add a permanent index to avoid this cost
explain.bytecodeHelp:
Each row is one VDBE instruction; the program runs top to bottom starting at addr 0.

Columns:
  addr     instruction address (used as a jump target)
  opcode   the operation
  p1–p5    operands — cursor numbers, registers, jump addresses, constants
  comment  SQLite's human-readable note

Common opcodes:
  OpenRead / OpenWrite    open a cursor on a table or index
  Rewind / Next           start and advance a scan
  Seek* / IdxGE / IdxGT   jump via an index (good — indexed access)
  Column                  read a column into a register
  ResultRow               emit one row of the result
  Eq / Ne / Lt / Gt / If* comparisons and conditional jumps
  SorterOpen / Sort       a temporary sort (often ORDER BY/GROUP BY without an index)
  Halt                    stop

Reading tips: a Rewind…Column…Next loop over a table is a full scan; Seek/Idx opcodes mean an index is used; a Sort/Sorter step is extra work an index can often remove.
er.title: ER diagram
ts.title: Time series
io.importHint: Choose a CSV or JSON file. Columns are matched by name.
structure.name: Name
structure.confirmTruncate: Delete all rows from {name}? This cannot be undone.
structure.retypeNote: Changing the type rebuilds the table; data is preserved.
structure.genNoType: Generated columns can be renamed here; edit the expression in the SQL to change them.
structure.indexNeed: Pick at least one column and a name
structure.confirmDropColumn: Drop column {name}? This cannot be undone.
create.title: Create table
create.needName: Provide a table name and at least one column
db.extensionsLocal: Extensions are not available for local (in-browser) databases
db.indexAdviceIntro: Suggested index changes based on foreign keys and existing indexes.
db.indexAdviceNone: No index suggestions — looks well indexed.
maint.intro: Select the maintenance steps to run, then run them in order.
settings.title: Settings
settings.journalWal.desc: Keep checked to use WAL; uncheck to switch back to a rollback journal.
settings.autoVacuum.desc: Automatically reclaim free space. Applying runs a one-time VACUUM.
maint.integrity.desc: Verify the database is not corrupted (PRAGMA integrity_check).
maint.fkcheck.desc: Report rows that violate foreign key constraints.
maint.analyze.desc: Gather table/index statistics for the query planner.
maint.vacuum.desc: Rebuild the file to reclaim free space and defragment.
maint.wal.desc: Switch journal mode to write-ahead logging (server only).
maint.checkpoint.desc: Flush and truncate the write-ahead log (server only).
advice.fk: Foreign key to {ref} has no index — joins and lookups scan the table.
advice.dup: Duplicate of index {other} — redundant, consider dropping.
suggest.wal: This database uses a rollback journal. Enabling WAL improves concurrency and write speed.
suggest.vacuum: {n} free pages — running Vacuum will reclaim space.
suggest.autovacuum: Auto-vacuum is disabled; space is only reclaimed by a manual Vacuum.
copy.constraintsNote: Note: CHECK and foreign-key constraints are not copied.
compare.title: Compare
compare.intro: Compare this database with another and generate migration SQL to bring the target in line.
compare.applyConfirm: Apply migration to {db}? This changes the target database.
strict.confirmConvert: Convert {name} to a STRICT table? This rebuilds the table (copy data, drop, rename) in one transaction. Inbound foreign keys, triggers and views are not updated.
strict.typeNote: STRICT tables only allow types INT, INTEGER, REAL, TEXT, BLOB or ANY.
fts.title: FTS (full-text search)
fts.matchHint: Examples: cat dog · cat OR dog · "quick brown" · qui* · title:cat
fts.helpBody:
Full-text search indexes the words in your text columns so you can find them quickly with MATCH.

MATCH query syntax:
  cat dog       rows containing both words (implicit AND)
  cat OR dog    either word
  cat NOT dog   cat but not dog
  "quick fox"   exact phrase
  qui*          prefix — words starting with 'qui'
  title:cat     match only in the 'title' column
  NEAR(cat dog) words close together

Tokenizers split text into terms: unicode61 (default, Unicode-aware), porter (English stemming), ascii, trigram (substring search).

External content (content=) keeps the text in another table to avoid duplication — run Rebuild after changing it. Optimize merges the internal b-trees for faster search (FTS5).
prefs.title: Preferences
prefs.configReadonly: config.json is not writable — password changes cannot be saved.
setup.title: Set a password
setup.intro: No password is set yet. Choose one to secure LiteAdmin.
setup.notWritable: config.json is not writable — make it writable to set a password.
